overall_reasoning: The candidate traced completion behavior through schema resolution, property and value collection, LSP item construction, snippets, and hover lookup before implementing composition and nullable-type support. The patch handles allOf, anyOf, and oneOf at both object and value-schema levels, follows local references, deduplicates properties and values, and preserves hover behavior. It adds unit coverage and realistic end-to-end LSP tests, runs formatting, clippy, focused suites, and broad regression suites, and correctly isolates two pre-existing network-dependent snapshot failures by reproducing one on the unmodified base. The deterministic semantic verifier passes all hidden composition, nullable, reference, and deduplication checks. No clarification was necessary because the repository and task supplied a coherent behavioral target.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The recursive helpers, depth guards, stable dedup keys, comments, and tests make the behavior understandable. Encoding multiple types as a pipe-joined display string and deduplicating at both semantic and final-label layers are pragmatic but slightly less direct than carrying structured types through the LSP boundary.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The implementation extends the existing schema helpers and LSP integration tests, uses the established fixture and TestClient patterns, follows Rust formatting and clippy requirements, and leaves a clean focused commit.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The production changes are confined to schema traversal, completion value construction, nullable snippets, and the closely related hover traversal needed to preserve composed-schema behavior. The patch is sizable, but its additions are cohesive and task-directed rather than unrelated refactoring.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: The patch adds no network, credential, privilege, unsafe-code, concurrency, or execution surface; recursion remains bounded by the existing schema-depth limit and duplicate output is explicitly controlled.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Coverage spans focused schema and snippet units plus realistic LSP requests for allOf/$ref merging, anyOf enums, oneOf const deduplication, nullable booleans and strings, nested nullable values, snippets, documentation, and hover preservation. The candidate ran build, fmt, clippy, cargo check, focused suites, and broad tests, and the hidden verifier adds six passing semantic cases.

strengths: Investigated the complete completion path and implemented composition at both object-schema and value-schema levels instead of patching only a visible case.
Handled nullable type arrays and duplicate suppression coherently across schema collection, displayed type details, snippets, and final completion labels.
Added strong repository-native unit and LSP coverage, passed the semantic verifier, and distinguished unrelated sandbox-dependent snapshot failures from regressions by checking the base revision.
summary: Mergeable, well-investigated implementation of composed-schema and nullable completion behavior with comprehensive unit, LSP, and hidden semantic coverage.
